Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(gsm): prevent arrow crossing between neighbors, thinner lines

Anti-crossing algorithm:
- Pre-compute angular position of each contact from subscriber
- Find minimum angular gap to nearest neighbor per contact
- Limit curve offset (maxBow) to stay within each contact's
  angular lane: len * sin(gap/2) * 0.4, capped at 16px
- Separate OUT/IN endpoints by ±3px perpendicular offset so
  they don't share the exact same start/end points

Thinner lines:
- Stroke-width reduced from 2.5px → 1.5px
- Arrowheads reduced from 10×8 → 7×5 to match
- Hover: 2.5px (was 3.5px)
- Filter range: 1.2–2px (was 2–3px)
- Legend lines: 1.5px (was 2px)

commit.message:
fix: switch SMTP to port 587 STARTTLS, add timeouts to prevent hanging on Render

Port 465 (direct SSL) is blocked on Render's outbound network causing
transporter.verify() to hang indefinitely. Switch all transporters to
port 587 with STARTTLS (secure:false, requireTLS:true) which is allowed.

Add connectionTimeout/greetingTimeout/socketTimeout of 10s each so failed
connections fail fast with a clear error instead of timing out the request.

Affected: routes/employer.js (scheduleMailer + test-email transporter),
          routes/auth.js (OTP transporter)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(cli): unify list and status commands (#215)

* fix: address adversarial review findings for PR #214

- Remove stale CLAUDE.md reference to "legacy portolan dataset command"
- Rename test_cli_dataset.py to test_cli_list.py (file tests portolan list)
- Add CHANGELOG entry for breaking change (dataset command removal)
- Clear stale pytest cache entries

Fixes issues identified in adversarial review:
1. CRITICAL: CLAUDE.md falsely stated dataset command exists
2. HIGH: Test file misnamed - doesn't test dataset commands anymore
3. HIGH: No CHANGELOG entry for breaking change

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(cli): unify list and status commands (#210)

Fold status into list command for cleaner UX:
- Add status indicators (✓ tracked, + untracked, ~ modified, ! deleted)
- Scan ALL files in catalog, not just geo-assets
- Add --tracked-only and --untracked-only filter flags
- Remove status command entirely (BREAKING)

The principle: everything in a catalog is tracked unless explicitly
excluded by config.yaml ignored_files patterns.

Also fixes:
- CPG sidecar files now detected as GeoParquet format
- Spaces in file extensions are now sanitized during scan --fix
- Tests updated for new unified behavior

Closes #210

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update integration tests for unified list command

Tests updated to reflect #210 changes:
- Replace status command references with list or config list
- Update expected output format (counts show status indicators)
- Update JSON output structure (collections with nested items)
- Remove test_status_integration.py (tests removed command)

commit.message:
fix: replace timestamp-based IDs with UUID v4 for notes and items (#13)

* fix: replace timestamp-based IDs with UUID v4 for notes and items

Note and ChecklistItem IDs were generated as 'note-{timestamp}' and
'item-{timestamp}'. PostgreSQL's uuid column type rejects these strings
with code 22P02, causing every createNote / updateNote call to fail in
production.

Replace both ID generation sites in _createNewChecklist (home_page.dart)
with Uuid().v4(). Also update FakeChecklistDataSource seed data to use
uuid.v4() so dev and prod use the same ID format, preventing this class
of bug from being hidden in local testing.

Fixes: PostgrestException code 22P02 on createNote / updateNote

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BGx6HqqjPSbym5FrUYp3F1

* fix: replace item timestamp ID with UUID v4 in checklist_detail_page

_addItem() was still generating item IDs as 'item-{timestamp}', causing
the same PostgrestException (code 22P02) on every updateNote call when
a new item is added inside the detail view.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BGx6HqqjPSbym5FrUYp3F1

* fix: replace all remaining timestamp IDs with UUID v4; fix upsert race

Non-UUID IDs fixed:
- category_form_dialog.dart: 'cat-{ms}' → Uuid().v4()
- reminder_picker_sheet.dart: 'reminder-{ms}' → Uuid().v4()

Duplicate key race fixed (supabase_checklist_datasource.dart):
- updateNote used delete-all + insert for checklist_items and
  note_assignees. Concurrent save calls (one per keystroke) would race:
  call A inserts rows, then call B tries to insert the same rows →
  23505 duplicate key violation.
- New strategy: delete only rows that were removed from the current set,
  then upsert the current set (onConflict: 'id' / 'note_id,actor_id').
  Concurrent calls now safely converge on the same final state.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BGx6HqqjPSbym5FrUYp3F1

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Dashboard redesign: adaptive operative terminal, Bureau Archives page, dashboard API

Replace static brochure dashboard with state-adaptive terminal layout:
- Command strip (44px status bar) replacing 340px hero
- Active Operations panel with dossier cards for epoch participations
- Split-frame grid layout (2-col desktop, 3-col widescreen, 1-col mobile)
- Featured shard hero card, compact resonance ticker, substrate ticker
- Boot sequence animation (session-gated), tactical grid background
- Persona-based rendering: guest/new_member/active_player/power_user

Add Bureau Archives page (/archives):
- Declassified dossier format for 23 lore sections across 6 chapters
- Sticky chapter navigation with IntersectionObserver scrollspy
- Pull quotes as intercepted transmissions (cosmic/signal/character variants)
- Evidence photographs with lightbox, deep link support (#section-id)
- Scroll-in animations with prefers-reduced-motion support

Backend: GET /api/v1/users/me/dashboard endpoint consolidating memberships,

commit.message:
Split generation into two-stage pipeline for better diagrams (#5)

Stage 1 uses tool_use to select tools, describe architecture, and
provide build guidance (no diagram). Stage 2 takes the architecture
description and generates focused Mermaid code. This decomposition
produces higher-quality diagrams since Claude can dedicate full
attention to Mermaid syntax in Stage 2.

Also extracts MERMAID_RULES constant and repairDiagram helper for
reuse across stages.

Inspired by gitdiagram's multi-stage pipeline insight
(github.com/ahmedkhaleel2004/gitdiagram).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
add OpenClaw security reputation and jawn-vault contrast

OpenClaw has known vulnerabilities (prompt injection, credential
exposure, permissive defaults). Our system uses GPG-encrypted pass
store + jawn-vault Rust service — no plaintext keys, no .env files,
credentials isolated behind Unix socket over Tailscale.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add per-user shell configs and shell tool installation (#4)

Adds local per-user shell configuration management, keyed by username from `--create-user` flag. When a user is created, matching configs from `configs/{username}/` are copied to the target host and deployed via a new Ansible role. Includes h3h user configs (.zshrc, .aliases, .zshfuncs, etc.) with oh-my-posh theme.

Installs shell tools unconditionally: oh-my-posh, zoxide, direnv.

Fixes rbenv shims in PATH and ensures zsh sources /etc/profile for all profile.d scripts.

commit.message:
feat: fix My Videos saving + add continue watching feature

## Bug fix: Video not saved to My Videos
- party/create: call POST /external-videos before creating room
  (deduplication on backend makes this idempotent)

## Continue watching
- content service: WatchProgress model (userId + videoUrl unique index)
- content service: watchProgress service/controller/routes
  (POST / → save, GET /?videoUrl= → get, POST /batch → getBatch)
- watch-party: createRoom accepts startTime, sets initial currentTime in room + Redis
- party page: save progress every 30s via interval, and on leave (owner only)
  uses handleVideoProgress to track currentTime and duration via onProgress callback
- UniversalPlayer: added onProgress prop, forwarded to VideoPlayer for direct videos
- my-videos: batch-fetch progress for all videos, show progress bar + time display
  'Davom etish' button resumes from saved position, replay icon restarts from 0
- web API proxy: /api/watch-progress route + /watch-progress rewrite in next.config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: align all documentation with codebase reality

Remove references to nonexistent features: bujo summary command,
bujo deleted/restore commands, and AI env vars (BUJO_AI_ENABLED,
BUJO_AI_PROVIDER, BUJO_MODEL, GEMINI_API_KEY) that were never
implemented. Replace AI_SETUP.md with accurate insights system docs.
Correct DATA.md persistence model from blanket event sourcing to
hybrid model. Update CLAUDE.md tech stack to Go 1.24 and current
adapter layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: fix benchmark OOM by splitting scenarios and bumping npm cache

The all-rules eslint scenario uses ~17 GB on date-fns and ~40 GB on
grafana — far exceeding CI runner memory (7 GB). Exit code 143 (SIGTERM)
from the OOM killer.

Fix:
- Split benchmark into two steps: equivalent (all corpora) and all-rules
  (express only, which fits at ~719 MB)
- Bump npm cache key to v2 to pick up newly required eslint plugins

commit.message:
Promote baryon asymmetry to DERIVED, fix q_0 error_pct, clean up stale comments

- Baryon asymmetry eta_B = J * alpha^2 * d/2^d upgraded from CONJECTURAL to DERIVED:
  each factor (J, alpha^2, d/2^d) now has clear geometric/perturbative justification.
  Result: 5.86e-10 vs observed 6.1e-10 (-4.0%). All 38 parameters now SOLID or DERIVED.
- Fix q_0 error_pct: was hardcoded abs(-0.5 - (-0.55)), now uses computed -1/(d-1).
- Replace stale simulation value 1.126 references with analytic 9/8 = (2^d+1)/2^d.

commit.message:
Add NaturalKeyDefinition to IAggregateProjection and fix EventTagQuery Or/AndEventsOfType chaining

Add NaturalKeyDefinition property to IAggregateProjection interface with
automatic discovery from [NaturalKey] and [NaturalKeySource] attributes
in JasperFxAggregationProjectionBase. This fixes build errors in Marten
and Polecat that reference projection.NaturalKeyDefinition.

Also fix EventTagQuery.AndEventsOfType to replace the preceding tag-only
condition from Or(tag) when chaining, so Or(tag).AndEventsOfType<A,B>()
produces the same result as Or<A,Tag>(tag).Or<B,Tag>(tag).

Make jump execute immediately on the frame the key is pressed

before landing).
